app.purpose: Organizational root for application-layer code split into two children: app/common (Google Wire DI wiring for all binaries) and app/config (shared Viper configuration structs). Contains no business logic; its sole constraint is that nothing in openmeter/* or pkg/* may import it.

patterns.name: Leaf-node import direction
patterns.description: app/common depends on all openmeter/* and pkg/* packages, never the reverse. app/config is imported by app/common and cmd/* only.
patterns.example: openmeter/billing imports nothing from app/; app/common/billing.go imports openmeter/billing

patterns.name: One child per concern
patterns.description: app/common owns runtime wiring (Wire provider sets, hook/validator registration, noop fallbacks); app/config owns pure data structs with Validate() and SetViperDefaults.
patterns.example: app/config/billing.go defines BillingConfig.Validate(); app/common/billing.go calls billing.New(adapter, ...) inside a Wire provider

key_file_guides.role: Wire provider set for billing domain; constructs billing.Service, BillingRegistry, charges adapters
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding business logic or state mutation inside provider functions; forgetting BillingRegistry.ChargesServiceOrNil() nil-check pattern

key_file_guides.role: Wires ledger services; returns noop implementations when credits.enabled=false
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any new ledger-touching provider that lacks a creditsConfig.Enabled guard returning a noop

key_file_guides.role: Registers customer service hooks and request validators from billing and ledger packages
key_file_guides.watch_for: Calling RegisterHooks or RegisterRequestValidator from domain packages instead of here (causes circular imports)

key_file_guides.role: Root config.Configuration struct used by all binaries; assembles all sub-configs
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding fields without a corresponding Validate() check and Configure* default

anti_patterns: Importing app/common from any openmeter/* or pkg/* package
Adding business logic (validation, computation, state mutation) inside app/common provider functions
Creating a ledger-backed provider without a credits.Enabled guard returning a noop
Calling customerService.RegisterHooks or RegisterRequestValidator from domain packages instead of app/common
Adding config fields to app/config structs without Validate() coverage and SetViperDefaults registration

decisions.decision: All DI wiring concentrated in app/common with plain constructors in domain packages
decisions.rationale: Compile-time Wire verification; single edit point to add a provider to any binary without duplicating graphs

decisions.decision: Credits feature guarded independently in each ledger-touching provider in app/common
decisions.rationale: Credits cross-cuts multiple unrelated call graphs (HTTP handlers, customer hooks, namespace provisioning); no single choke point can gate all of them

docs.purpose: Documentation-only folder split into ADRs (docs/decisions/) recording immutable architectural choices and user-facing migration guides (docs/migration-guides/) for breaking changes. Neither subfolder contains Go source; changes here affect operator understanding, not compile-time behavior.

patterns.name: ADR immutability
patterns.description: Existing ADR files in docs/decisions/ record a past decision and must not be edited to change that decision; create a new sequentially numbered ADR instead.
patterns.example: 0005-clickhouse.md documents the switch from ksqlDB; a reversal would require 0006-*.md

patterns.name: Migration guide per breaking change
patterns.description: Each file in docs/migration-guides/ covers exactly one breaking change with SQL audit queries before destructive statements, Before/After API examples, and a deprecation timeline table.
patterns.example: 2025-08-12-subject-customer-consolidation.md covers the subject→customer consolidation with SQL scripts and a phased removal timeline

key_file_guides.role: ADR: choice of Kafka as the event streaming platform
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not edit to reflect a different decision; create 000N-*.md

key_file_guides.role: ADR: ClickHouse replaces ksqlDB for analytics
key_file_guides.watch_for: This ADR is the authoritative source for why ClickHouse is the analytics store

key_file_guides.role: Operator guide: autoMigrate modes (ent, migration, false) and Atlas workflow
key_file_guides.watch_for: Keep in sync with actual atlas.hcl and Makefile targets

key_file_guides.role: User-facing explanation of CloudEvents format, JSONPath meter config, and deduplication semantics
key_file_guides.watch_for: Any change to ingest API contract must be reflected here

anti_patterns: Editing existing ADR files to change the recorded decision — write a new numbered ADR
Combining multiple unrelated breaking changes in a single migration guide file
Omitting the 'Considered Options' section when writing a new ADR
Using docs/decisions for operational runbooks or migration SQL — those belong in docs/migration-guides
Writing a migration guide without audit SELECT queries before destructive UPDATE/DELETE statements

decisions.decision: ADRs in docs/decisions/ are append-only historical records
decisions.rationale: Immutable records prevent revisionist history and provide a clear audit trail of why the current architecture exists

decisions.decision: SQL migration scripts embedded directly in docs/migration-guides/ rather than as separate .sql files
decisions.rationale: Self-contained guides are easier for operators to follow without cross-referencing separate files

quickstart.purpose: Self-contained demo environment that boots all OpenMeter services via Docker Compose and validates end-to-end ingest→query behavior with quickstart_test.go. It is the canonical smoke-test for new operators and uses port range 40000–49999 to avoid conflicts with the main dev environment.

patterns.name: OPENMETER_ADDRESS env-gate on tests
patterns.description: Every test helper calls t.Skip if OPENMETER_ADDRESS is unset; tests are never run against an embedded server, only against a live Docker Compose stack.
patterns.example: func initClient(t *testing.T) { if address == "" { t.Skip("OPENMETER_ADDRESS not set") } }

patterns.name: require.EventuallyWithT for async ingest assertions
patterns.description: All ingest-then-query assertions use assert.EventuallyWithT with a 30-second timeout and 1-second tick to tolerate Kafka/ClickHouse propagation latency.
patterns.example: assert.EventuallyWithT(t, func(t *assert.CollectT) { resp := queryMeter...; require.Len(t, resp.JSON200.Data, 2) }, 30*time.Second, time.Second)

patterns.name: Port range 40000–49999
patterns.description: All Docker Compose service ports in this folder use the 40000 range to avoid collisions with the root dev docker-compose.yaml which binds standard ports.

patterns.name: Separate debug-ports overlay
patterns.description: docker-compose.debug-ports.yaml adds host port bindings for Kafka/ClickHouse/Redis/Postgres/Svix only when debugging; base docker-compose.yaml exposes only application-level ports.
patterns.example: make test-local merges both files: docker compose -f docker-compose.yaml -f docker-compose.debug-ports.yaml

key_file_guides.role: End-to-end smoke test: creates meters, ingests CloudEvents, asserts meter query values for both v1 and v3 APIs
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses context.Background() in helper functions (acceptable here as integration test — not application code); uses api/client/go and api/v3 generated clients directly

key_file_guides.role: Full-stack compose file referencing docker-compose.base.yaml services; declares all five OpenMeter binaries as services
key_file_guides.watch_for: New binaries added to the main stack must also be added here with matching command and config volume mount

key_file_guides.role: Minimal production-like config for the quickstart stack; uses autoMigrate: migration (not ent)
key_file_guides.watch_for: If new required config fields are added to app/config, this file must be updated or the quickstart stack will fail to start

key_file_guides.role: Defines test-local target: spin up stack, health-check sink-worker, run tests, tear down
key_file_guides.watch_for: Health check hits sink-worker port 40000 (not the openmeter API port 48888)

anti_patterns: Using port numbers outside the 40000 range — they conflict with the root docker-compose.yaml dev environment
Running quickstart_test.go without a live Docker Compose stack — tests must guard with t.Skip on missing OPENMETER_ADDRESS
Hardcoding event IDs or meter slugs without a unique suffix — parallel test runs collide on shared meter state
Omitting depends_on health checks when adding a new service to docker-compose.yaml — services start before dependencies are ready

decisions.decision: quickstart_test.go uses the generated Go SDK clients (api/client/go, api/v3) rather than raw HTTP for v1 but raw HTTP for v3
decisions.rationale: Demonstrates the intended SDK usage to new users while covering the v3 API surface that lacks a fully generated client helper at the time of writing
Production-like behavior: exercises the Atlas-generated SQL migration path rather than the development-only ent.Schema.Create upsertion

code_examples.scenario: Assert meter query value after async ingest using EventuallyWithT
